other_observers: Larry Semo, Joey Kellner, Glenn Walbek, Rachel Hopper, Ira Sanders, Andrew Spencer, Loch Kilpatrick, and Steve Stachowiak.; Larry Semo found the bird while the group mentioned in "other observers" was waiting for a Pine Warbler to show up at Hopper's feeder.
description: Small, dark, stocky goose associating with Cackling and Canada geese. The plumage was mostly blackish, including the chest and belly, except for the silvery-white flanks (with vertical dark striping), bold white triangular patches on sides of neck, and white upper- and undertail coverts. Bill, legs, and eyes were also black. The bird was a little smaller than the other geese in close proximity, which I thought were probably Lesser Canada Goose (C. b. parvipes). In flight, the short, black tail surrounded by white upper- and undertail coverts was obvious.Behaviors: Mostly sat on ice in association with a large flock of "white-cheeked" geese, occasionally stood, and eventually flew off.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
